$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `plugin_config` repo (Phase-2). One
committed file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the v4 oracle (build-plugin-config-fixture.ts + plugin-config-tier2.ts)
and
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness plugin_config tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: a user-scoped `userId` column, the open / arbitrary-JSON
object column (`config`, z.record(z.string(), z.unknown())), and an OPTIONAL
boolean with NO default (`enabled`, z.boolean().optional()) -> INTEGER 0/1
when present, SQL NULL when absent.
OPEN-JSON CONSTRAINT (tracked deferred seam, #5 in phase-2-onramp.md): every
`config` value is either {} or a SINGLE-KEY object, so v4's JSON.stringify
(insertion order) and Rust's serde_json::Value (sorted keys) serialize
byte-identically. A multi-key object would diverge — see the module header in
plugin_config.rs. The fractional value 0.5 exercises js_number_to_json's float
pass-through inside the JSON text.
ENABLED-ABSENT: a seed row and a create op OMIT the `enabled` key entirely.
v4 stores no value for the optional-no-default column -> the cell is SQL NULL.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / image_profiles fixtures use. It is NOT the real Friday
pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New config with a MULTI-KEY config object whose keys are in NON-SORTED order (zeta, alpha, maxResults). Proves the open-JSON column preserves v4's JSON.stringify INSERTION order (serde_json preserve_order) rather than sorting keys -- sorted order would be alpha, maxResults, zeta. Also exercises js_number_to_json float pass-through.
New config with empty {} config and enabled ABSENT (omit the key -> stored SQL NULL).
Update seed row 1: replace the single-key config object. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row 2: set enabled (NULL -> true). updatedAt pinned.
